title: Oregon Health Insurance Experiment — Medicaid Coverage Effects
description: Effect sizes of Medicaid coverage vs no coverage on 4 health outcomes from the Oregon Health Insurance Experiment (Finkelstein et al., QJE 2012). Depression screening shows the largest positive effect (+30%).

L2_specific: impact of Medicaid expansion on health outcomes in low-income populations
effet de la couverture Medicaid sur les résultats de santé
randomized experiment comparing insured vs uninsured populations
L3_detailed: depression screening increased by 30% under Medicaid coverage in the Oregon experiment
le dépistage de la dépression a montré le plus grand effet positif de la couverture Medicaid
Medicaid increased emergency visits and depression detection but showed no significant physical health improvement
